United Kingdom

Historic · Iconic · Eccentric

Key facts

CapitalLondon
Population67.7M
LanguageEnglish
National sportFootball / Cricket
National dishFish & Chips

About United Kingdom

United Kingdom is a country. Its capital city is London, with other major cities including Manchester and Birmingham. With a population of approximately 67.7M, the main language spoken is English.

Did you know

The UK gave the world the World Wide Web, the telephone, television, the steam engine, and the passenger railway.

Explore United Kingdom

🏛

Capital

London serves as the political, cultural and economic heart of United Kingdom. As the seat of government and often the most populous city, it concentrates the country's main institutions, universities and cultural landmarks. Beyond the capital, major cities include Manchester, Birmingham, Edinburgh — each a hub of regional culture, economy and history. London has been continuously occupied since Roman Londinium was founded around 50 CE, and the City of London — the original square mile — still operates under a medieval corporation with its own Lord Mayor, separate from the wider London mayor, governing a financial district whose legal architecture predates Parliament.

👥

People

With a population of approximately 67.7M, United Kingdom is a vibrant society with a rich mix of traditions and communities. The official language is English, which reflects the country's cultural heritage and connects it with a wide international community. British understatement is not merely a conversational style but a codified social technology: the phrase 'not bad' signals genuine enthusiasm, 'quite good' is moderate praise, and 'interesting' usually means the opposite — a system that functions perfectly within the culture and baffles everyone outside it.

🍽

Food

The emblematic dish of United Kingdom is Fish & Chips. Fish and Chips emerged as a working-class staple in the industrial north and east of England in the 1860s, when railway refrigeration made fresh sea fish available inland for the first time — by 1910 there were 25,000 chip shops across the country, feeding factory workers on Friday pay nights.

🏅

Sport

Football / Cricket holds a special place in the heart of United Kingdom's national identity. England invented association football, codified it at a Cambridge pub meeting in 1863, exported it to every corner of the globe, and then waited 103 years to win a World Cup — the 1966 triumph at Wembley, sealed by Geoff Hurst's hat-trick, remains the national sporting reference point against which everything else is measured.

⛰

Nature

The highest point in United Kingdom is Ben Nevis, rising to 1,345 metres above sea level. The Scottish Highlands contain Europe's most extensive sub-arctic plateau in the Cairngorms, a landscape of ancient Caledonian pine forest, ptarmigan, red squirrel and osprey, shaped by ice sheets that retreated only 10,000 years ago and now one of the last places in Britain where the land has not been fundamentally altered by agriculture.

Major cities
